# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

class FilesController < ApplicationController
    def download_file
        # SECURE (Do this) - Resolve requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use, reject absolute paths etc... 
        
        allowed_dir = Rails.root.join('uploads')   # Define the root of your upload folder as 'uploads' in config/routes.rb or any other way you prefer to set this up (e.g., /public)
      
        filename =  params[:filename]  # Get file name from request parameters, e.g.: "user_documents/sample1234567890"  
        
        path = File.join(allowed_dir, sanitize_and_validate(filename)) if allowed? filename    # Join the user's requested file name with your base directory and validate it (SECURE)  or reject otherwise...    
      
      unless Rails.application.config.consider_all_requests_secure_by_default   # If not in production mode, then we can use allowlisted identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths ...   
          path = sanitize(filename)  if allowed? filename     # Sanitizing the file name (INSECURE), only when it's an identifier that is inside your list...  
       end     
        
        send_file(path, type: 'application/pdf', disposition: 'attachment')    # Send user-uploaded files via Rails controller action using File.send_file method  (SECURE) ...    
         
           private def sanitize_and_validate filename   # Define a helper function to canonicalise and validate the file path...     
             return nil unless allowed?(filename)    # Reject if not in list of allowlisted identifiers, SECURITY REQUIREMENTS (Canonicalization & Validation...) ...    
           sanitize_and_validate filename.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9\-]/, '').split('.').first   # Remove any non alphanumeric characters and split the string by dot to get only file name...    end  def allowed?(filename)    
             return false unless Rails.application.config.allowlisted_identifiers     
            !Rails.application.config.allowlisted_identifiers.include! filename   # Check if requested path is in allowlist, SECURITY REQUIREMENTS (Allow List...) ...    end  def sanitize(filename)     return nil unless allowed?(filename)      
             SanitizedFilename = ActiveSupport::Inflector.parameterize(File.basename filename).gsub(/[^a-z0-9\s\-]/i, '')   # Remove any non alphanumeric characters and replace spaces with hyphen...    end  def send_file path    
             return nil unless File.exist? (path)      Rails.logger.info "File not found: '#{filename}'" && raise("Not Found") if !Rails.application.config.consider_all_requests_secure   # If in production mode, then we can use allowlisted identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths ...    end
        rescue => e     Rails.logger.error "Error: '#{e}'"  return false unless File.exist? (path)      raise("Not Found") if !Rails.application.config.consider_all_requests_secure   # If in production mode, then we can use allowlisted identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths ...    end
end
